purpose: Current-phase task checklist generated from the agent-maintained source coverage matrix and platform capability check. Use it before editing and update the matrix/check after work.
instructions: Start with implementationGroups as the current phase implementation contract: implement each group, then use mustComplete entries as exact row-level checks.

Use planContract as upstream planning ownership evidence; resolve its alignmentFindings by updating plannedPhase/matrix/platform evidence or by documenting sourceOverride/analysisGap when source inspection disagrees.
Keep grouped business behavior discoverable through precise target endpoints; avoid closing unrelated features only through root/shell/whole-file endpoints.
Merge source files only when it improves target clarity and still preserves per-feature evidence, tests, and controllable file size.
Treat mustComplete entries as the current phase's executable row-level task list.
Use behaviorAudit as the current phase behavior audit; it checks closed critical/high rows for source behavior, target behavior, parity, and verification evidence.

Use uiContract entries as static-analysis planning aids, not complete UI specifications: preserve listed source-visible screen structure, route/back behavior, actions, state feedback, and visual evidence needs, then re-read source/resources/navigation for UI behavior the static analysis may have missed.
Do not close the phase while current-phase critical/high entries are open, weakly deferred, or missing evidence.
For every closed critical/high source feature, record sourceBehavior, targetBehavior, parityLevel, targetEndpoints, and verificationEvidence in source-coverage-matrix.json.
A build pass alone is not per-feature verification; cite tests, manual/emulator evidence, or explicit verificationEvidence for behavior closure.
Use implemented-simplified only with concrete simplification reason, preserved-behavior explanation, target endpoints or tests, and source/platform evidence.

If a suitable HarmonyOS/Cangjie API, Kit, example, or package exists for a platform capability, use it or record a platform replacement; do not close the item with local mock data, sample data, in-memory simulation, or another fallback.
Use deferred only for concrete blockers; otherwise implement faithfully or platform-replace with evidence.
Update source-coverage-matrix.json/md and platform-capability-check.json/md before phase completion.
If this checklist is empty but the phase has real scope in plan.md/plan.json, assign plannedPhase values first.

implementationGroups.title: Source behavior: presentation/login
requiredBehaviors: Source review of LoginScreen.kt found only static social-login icons rendered as tappable affordances; the screen does not open a bitmap viewer, gallery preview, zoom surface, or EXIF/media flow.

LoginScreen triggers a successful transition to `Screen.Home.route` from the login button callback, popping the start destination so onboarding/login are cleared from the stack instead of remaining on back navigation.
Source review of LoginScreen.kt found no notification publishing, widget update, RemoteViews, or home-screen integration behavior; the file only renders the login form and placeholder actions.

LoginScreen observes Compose state from `viewModel.email` and `viewModel.password`, and it collects `eventFlow` in `LaunchedEffect` so text changes and failed-login snackbar events recompose immediately while the screen stays active.

Source review of LoginScreen.kt found no runtime permission request, permission callback, or protected platform API access; the login flow uses only local text entry, toasts, snackbar messaging, and persisted login-state mutation through the view model.

LoginScreen performs screen-entry setup by creating scaffold state, wiring a `LaunchedEffect(key1 = true)` collector for one-shot UI events, and applying status/navigation bar colors while the login screen is active.

Successful login triggers persisted session-state mutation through `LoginViewModel` and `LoginRepository.toggleLoginState()`, so the screen changes a stored login preference rather than only local UI state.

OnBoarding coordinates pager state, window color styling, and login navigation only; it does not request permissions, process permission results, or use protected APIs.
OnBoarding performs route-entry setup by creating coroutine and pager state, loading slide items, and applying onboarding-specific status/navigation bar colors while the onboarding screen is active.
BottomSection renders only onboarding indicators and a trailing next button. It does not request permissions or access protected APIs.
Indicators and Indicator render animated onboarding progress pills only. They do not request permissions or touch protected APIs.

featureContracts.targetRequiredBehavior: The HarmonyOS onboarding route preserves the same observable page-state contract with `@State onboardingPageIndex` updated from the swiper `onChange` callback, and that state directly drives indicator selection plus last-page navigation behavior.
verificationEvidence: Source review: D:/Kotlin2Cangjie/Foodike/app/src/main/java/com/example/foodike/presentation/onboarding/OnBoarding.kt passes `pagerState.currentPage` into `BottomSection`, and that value controls both indicators and next-button branching.

Target review: entry/src/main/cangjie/ohos_app_cangjie_entry/app/bootstrap_page.cj updates `onboardingPageIndex` from the swiper `onIndexChanged` callback and branches on `onboardingStore.isLastPage(this.onboardingPageIndex)` in the next handler.
